name: Chocolate Berries or Orange Sections
recipeIngredient: Perfect strawberries or long stemmed berries from a fruit market
recipeInstructions:
1. Select perfect strawberries with fresh green tops, or better yet, the long stemmed berries sold in many fruit markets.
2. Thoroughly wash and dry the berries on paper towels.
3. Discard any soft-spotted berries as they will cause the finished product to look unappealing.
4. Melt about 6 ounces of high-quality bittersweet chocolate (e.g., Belgian, French, Lindt, or Tobler).
5. Line cookie sheets with waxed paper.
6. Pour melted chocolate into a dish that provides depth rather than surface area.
7. Dip each berry by the leaves into the chocolate, covering about 3/4 of it, leaving stems and some of the berry showing.
8. Allow excess chocolate to drip back into the bowl, then place the dipped berry onto the lined cookie sheet.
9. Refrigerate until the chocolate hardens.
10. For added elegance, melt white chocolate and dip the bottom of the dark chocolate berry or use a pastry bag with a fine tip to create decorative lines on the dark chocolate.
11. Serve on non-toxic green leaves or palm fronds from a florist for an elegant presentation.
